fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Effective Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date from which the insurance policy is valid.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date to which the insurance policy is valid.

fields.name: Policy No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the insurance policy number as stated on the actual insurance policy.

fields.name: Annual Premium
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the annual insurance premium.

fields.name: Policy Coverage
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the fixed asset is insured at the right value.

fields.name: Total Value Insured
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total value of fixed assets linked to this insurance policy. This is the value of fixed assets for which insurance is required.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether any comments have been entered for this insurance policy.
To view a comment or create a new one, click Related Information, point to Insurance, and then click Comment. A comment sheet window will appear, in which you can see the existing comments and create new ones. See also the Comment Line table.
You can also enter a comment in the Notes FactBox.

fields.name: Insurance Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of insurance (for example, theft or fire) that is covered by this insurance policy.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the insurance card was last modified.

fields.name: Insurance Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the vendor from whom you purchased this insurance policy.

fields.name: FA Class Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a fixed asset class code to assign to the insurance policy.

fields.name: FA Subclass Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a fixed asset subclass code to assign to the insurance policy.

fields.name: FA Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the location of the fixed asset(s) linked to the insurance policy.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code for a dimension. To see the dimension value codes you have set up for this dimension in the Dimension Value window, click the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, the program will use the corresponding dimension value as a default each time you enter the account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions and you can change them on the entry line, for example in a journal.
For more information about dimensions, see Dimension.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code for a dimension. To see the dimension value codes you have set up for this dimension in the Dimension Value window, click the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, the program will use the corresponding dimension value as a default each time you enter the account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions and you can change them on the entry line, for example in a journal.
For more information about dimensions, see Dimension.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code of the location of the fixed asset(s) linked to the insurance policy. You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters.
To see the location codes in the Location table, click the field.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the related record is blocked from being posted in transactions, for example a customer that is declared insolvent or an item that is placed in quarantine.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the insurance policy.

fields.name: Search Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a search description for the insurance policy.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains the starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval. For example, the Total Value Insured per FA window shows amounts for that interval only.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Contains the code for the number series that was used to assign the insurance policy's number.
